Title: Housing Resources | SF Planning

URL Source: https://sfplanning.org/housing-resources

Markdown Content:
The Housing Resource guide is for individuals experiencing homelessness or housing insecurity. Use this list to find Homeless Services, Rental Services & Eviction Assistance, Tenant Rights, and Affordable Housing in San Francisco. This list was created in partnership with community organizations and the [Mayor’s Office of Housing and Community Development.](https://sf.gov/departments/mayors-office-housing-and-community-development "website")

The Housing Resource guide is available in seven languages:

_The services and resources provided are subject to change. To report a correction or request to be added, please contact: [CPC.CommunityPlanning@sfgov.org](mailto:CPC.CommunityPlanning@sfgov.org)_

* * *

**Homeless Services & Resources**
[**Homeless Shelters**](https://sfserviceguide.org/shelter-resources/form "website")

Find a shelter for you or your family’s needs with this list. You can filter by age, household size and more.

_Available languages: English, Español, Filipino, 中文_

[**Coordinated Entry, Department of Homelessness & Supportive Housing**](https://hsh.sfgov.org/services/how-to-get-services/referrals-and-housing-assistance/ "website")

If you are at risk of or currently experiencing homelessness, contact a Coordinated Entry Access Point to get assistance. Access Point staff provide problem solving support and assess your needs.

_Available languages: English, Español, 中文,Filipino, العربية_

[**Shelter Client Advocacy**](https://evictiondefense.org/services/shelter-client-advocacy/ "website")

For assistance with a Denial of Service (DOS) or an issue at a City-funded homeless shelter or transitional housing site, contact the [Eviction Defense Collaborative](https://evictiondefense.org/ "website") at (415) 669-0284 or email [sca@evictiondefense.org](mailto:sca@evictiondefense.org "website").

_Available languages: English, Español, 中文_

[**Essential Services in SF**](https://sfserviceguide.org/ "website")

The SF Service Guide is a central website to get connected to food, health, showers and other services shelters. Find long-term housing, rental assistance, financial assistance, jobs, LGBTQ+ and/or Domestic Violence Survivor resources and more.

_Available languages: English, Español, Filipino, 中文_

[**Quality of Life Citation Support**](https://www.cohsf.org/ "website")

Bring a copy of your citation to any of the addresses listed below:

*   **GLIDE:**330 Ellis (at Jones) – Monday and Thursday 2:00pm-5:00pm
*   **Hospitality House:**290 Turk (at Leavenworth) – Monday 2:30pm-4:00pm, Wednesday 10:00am-12:00pm, Thursday 11:00am-1:00pm
*   **St Boniface:**133 Golden Gate Ave – Monday- Friday 6:00am-2:00pm, Except Tuesdays from 6:00am-1:00pm
*   **Church of St John the Evangelist:**1661 15th Street – Monday-Friday 6:00am-12:00pm
*   **Hospitality House Employment Resource Center:**181 6th Street–Friday 9:00am-12:00pm

You may pick up a copy of your citation at 850 Bryant Street, Room 145. To follow-up on the status of your citation call (415) 551- 8550 with your citation number. For MUNI citations, go to the SFMTA Customer Service Center at 11 South Van Ness. For more resources, call the Coalition on Homelessness at (415) 346-3740.

**Rental Assistance, Eviction & General Resources**
[**SF Emergency Rental Assistance Program**](https://sferap.com/ "website")

Also known as SF ERAP, this program aids eligible San Francisco residents who have past-due rent or need help paying the costs of moving into a new unit they have already identified and plan to lease. Applicants are eligible for assistance one time per calendar year.

_Available languages: English, Español, 中文_

[**Eviction Defense Collaborative**](https://evictiondefense.org/ "website")

If you have received eviction papers from the court, immediately contact the legal assistance line at (415) 659-9184 or email at [legal@evictiondefense.org](mailto:legal@evictiondefense.org "website"). Find support with eviction assistance, rental assistance, shelter client advocacy and tenant’s right to counsel.

_Available languages: English, Español, 中文_

[**Mayor’s Office of Housing and Community Development Housing Resource Guide**](https://sfmohcd.org/sites/default/files/Documents/MOH/SF%20Housing%20Resource%20Guide.pdf#:~:text=Drop-in%20or%20call-in%20%28415-703-8644%29%20counseling%20available%20Monday-Thursday%201-5PM.,tenants%20need%20to%20make%20an%20appointment%20at%20415-703-8634. "website")

Use this comprehensive guide to find housing and homelessness services you need. The guide is organized by resources specific to Veterans, Families, the Elderly, Youth, Individuals with Disabilities, HIV/AIDS, Domestic Violence Survivors and more.

_Available languages: English_

[**Rent Relief and Eviction Prevention**](https://sfadc.org/ "website")

The San Francisco Anti Displacement Coalition is a group of tenant organizations and allies who organize against evictions and illegal rent increases.

_Available languages: English, Español_

[**SF Rent Board**](https://sf.gov/departments/rent-board "website")

The San Francisco Rent Board protects tenants from excessive rent increases and unjust evictions while assuring landlords fair and adequate rents.

_Available languages: English, Español, Filipino, 中文_

**Tenant Rights**
[**Know Your Rights**](https://baylegal.org/resources/housing-kyr/ "website")

Bay Area Legal Aid provides four videos in 3 languages with need-to-know information for tenants including how to challenge your eviction, your basic rights, housing discrimination and housing protections.

_Available languages: English, Español, 中文_

[**Legal Services**](https://baylegal.org/what-we-do/ "website")

Call Bay Area Legal Aid at 1-800-551-554 a free legal hotline available to qualified low-income residents living in the Bay Area. The Legal Advice Line provides counsel and advice in all languages on a range of civil legal issues.

_Available languages: English, Español, 中文_

[**SF Tenants Union**](https://sftu.org/ "website")

The SF Tenants Union offers counseling, legal advice and tenants’ rights resources to empower tenants to learn and assert their rights under local, state, and federal law.

_Available languages: English, Español_

[**Unlawful Detainers & Repairs Resources**](https://www.tenantstogether.org/resources/repairs "website")

Causa Justa has a list of bilingual counseling hotlines in San Francisco and Oakland to assist tenants in defending their housing rights, like seeking repairs or responding to evictions.

_Available languages: English, Español_

**Affordable Housing Access**
[**Affordable Housing Listings for Seniors & Adults with Disabilities**](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1bJq_sBfHlTzMFyYmuaC0C-D-2C8Ukb-YpnlTKq_YPWI/edit "website")

Find immediately available affordable housing for LGBTQ+ individuals, seniors or adults with a disability.

_Available languages: English_

[**Apply for Affordable Housing with SF**](https://housing.sfgov.org/ "website")

Apply for 100% affordable housing with the City of San Francisco or get assistance from a housing counselor, language support or view additional housing options.

_Available languages: English, Español, Filipino, 中文_

[**Connect with a Housing Counselor**](https://housing.sfgov.org/housing-counselors "website")

Talk with a local housing counselor specific to your needs.

_Available languages: English, Español, 中文, Filipino, العربية, русский_

[**Find Long-Term Housing in SF**](https://sfserviceguide.org/longterm-housing-resources/form "website")

The SF Service Guide’s long-term housing form will connect you to a list of resources after answering a few questions on your housing situation.

_Available languages: English, Español, Filipino, 中文_

[**Home Match**](https://covia.org/programs/home-match/ "website")

Free home-sharing program that connects homeowners and tenants looking to rent a room for an affordable price or helping with household activities in exchange for lower costs.

_Available languages: English, Español, 中文_

[**Rental Opportunities**](https://sf.gov/information/san-francisco-rental-opportunities "website")

View listings and apply for rental housing opportunities on the DAHLIA San Francisco Housing Portal, Below Market Rate Rentals, Emergency Vouchers and other programs.

_Available languages: English, Español, Filipino, 中文_

[**Section 8 Voucher Information**](https://www.hrcsf.org/tenants-rights "website")

The Housing Rights Committee of SF created a guide providing information and how to get assistance with Section 8 Vouchers.

_Available languages: English, Español, Filipino, 中文_
